opp.name: Checkers - Rally's_ Opp - February '26
opp.next_step: 6/22/26 GK: awaiting RFP release first week of July 6/4/26 GK: Leslie response - S2P release pushed to beginning of Q3, we will still be included 5/26/26 GK: follow up with all 3 contacts at C&R, status of SIM award and S2P RFP release 4/13/26 GK: connecting with Checkers team on release date/timeline of S2P RFP 3/30/26 GK: S2P RFP release end of April 3/17/26 GK: followed up regarding SIM integrations and commitment to S2P rfp in following month 3/12/26 GK: connect with SC team - fitment gaps too high, no longer responding to this RFP. but staying engaged with C&R for immiment S2P RFP release in 1 month. sharing integration capabilities with Supplier Incident Mgmt solutions 3/2/26 GK: RFP received. beginning submission and coordinating necessary next steps 2/26/26 GK: demo occurred. rfp release 3/2, plans to release full S2P rfp in May 2026 2/23/26 GK: prep call w/ Srishti Tuesday 2/17/26 GK: scheduling pre-rfp demo for 2/26

items.quote: 0 OpportunityContactRole records despite 3 named buyer attendees on the 26 Feb 2026 demo
awaiting RFP release first week of July
status of SIM award and S2P RFP release

sources: SF: no MEDDPICC_2_0__c record found for this opportunity
metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated clear qualitative pain but never quantified it. On the 26 Feb 2026 demo call: 'Stores report incidents via web-based task system that emails a predefined chain; replies outside system email are lost,' and 'Incident and supplier performance tracked manually in Excel scorecards until system implementation.' No cost, headcount, cycle-time, or error-rate figures were stated on the call or in any Salesforce field (no MEDDPICC 2.0 record exists for this opportunity). Quantifying the cost of manual Excel tracking and lost incident replies is the move that would convert this into a hard business case.

champion.narrative: No individual shows demonstrated internal-advocacy evidence (executive introductions, pushing back on rival point solutions, or driving internal alignment). Leslie McElhaney is the closest candidate: she owned the action item to 'distribute the Supplier Incident Management RFP via Symphony eSourcing' (Task, 26 Feb 2026) and, per Grace Kim's 4 Jun 2026 Next Step note, personally confirmed the S2P release timing and that 'we will still be included' - a coordinating role, not demonstrated internal selling. Gina Rendar 'expressed prior positive impression of Zycus and interest in revisiting full suite' on the 26 Feb 2026 call but has not been heard from since. Neither has a confirmed title in Salesforce. The move is to confirm Leslie's actual role and test her willingness to introduce a senior sponsor.

competition.narrative: No named vendor competitor appears in any source: Competitors__c is blank and no Avoma call or Next_Step entry names a rival. The only competitive signal is SF's Why_not_Zycus__c field, which characterizes the RFP evaluation as facing a 'competitive vendor landscape with specialized point solutions' under strict cost/timeline scoring with third-party advisor Maine Pointe overseeing it. More materially, Zycus itself self-eliminated from the first-phase Supplier Incident Management RFP on 12 Mar 2026 citing 'fitment gaps too high' (Next_Step__c), while staying engaged for the larger S2P opportunity. The strongest current threat is not a named rival but this unresolved self-assessed fit gap recurring when the S2P RFP is scored, plus the buyer's status-quo manual process as a 'do nothing' alternative.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer's operational pain is specific and consistent across sources. SF's Customer_Business_Problem__c states: 'Restaurants receive out of specification product deliveries,' 'No centralized supplier incident management system,' 'Incident reporting lacks structured ticketing workflow,' and 'Feedback loop between restaurants and supplier teams is fragmented.' This was corroborated live on the 26 Feb 2026 demo: 'Stores report incidents via web-based task system that emails a predefined chain; replies outside system email are lost,' and 'Incident and supplier performance tracked manually in Excel scorecards until system implementation.' The pain owner is the supply chain / quality-control function represented by Josh Ullrich.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, legal, or procurement mechanics have surfaced in the demo call, Next_Step history, or any queried Salesforce field; the deal remains at Qualified stage with the S2P RFP not yet confirmed released. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null (no incumbent contract on file). This is expected for the current stage; visibility will only emerge once the S2P RFP process concludes and moves to vendor selection.

sources: SF Account Contact records: Chris Ward, Michael Blair, Minh Le
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named anywhere in the record. Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both false on the Opportunity, and there is no MEDDPICC 2.0 record for this deal. SF's Compelling_Event__c field notes 'clear executive sponsorship from initiative leaders' but names no one. The only two confirmed buyer contacts across more than four months are operational: Josh Ullrich (Supply Chain Manager, confirmed SF contact) and Leslie McElhaney (named on the demo Task and in Next_Step_History, title unconfirmed). Three C-level Checkers contacts exist in Salesforce at the account level (Chris Ward, Chief Supply Chain Officer; Michael Blair, Chief Financial & Strategy Officer; Minh Le, CIO) but none has been confirmed engaged on this specific opportunity. The move is to ask Josh or Leslie directly, at RFP release, who signs off on the S2P investment.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a two-phase RFP process. Phase one, the Supplier Incident Management (SIM) RFP, launched around 2 Mar 2026 (Next_Step__c); Zycus voluntarily withdrew from it on 12 Mar 2026 citing 'fitment gaps too high.' Phase two, the larger Source-to-Pay (S2P) suite RFP, has slipped three times: from 'end of April' (30 Mar 2026 note) to 'May' (per the 26 Feb 2026 demo expectation) to 'beginning of Q3' (Leslie McElhaney's confirmed reply, 4 Jun 2026), and is now overdue against the 'first week of July' target referenced in Grace Kim's 22 Jun 2026 follow-up. Third-party advisor Maine Pointe is formally engaged to run the evaluation, and RFPs are distributed via the Symphony eSourcing platform. No confirmed step-by-step approval chain beyond RFP submission has been shared.

decision_criteria.narrative: SF's evaluation-process field (Why_not_Zycus__c) states the RFP is 'highly structured... with external advisor oversight' from the relevant stakeholder, scored on 'cost and timeline criteria,' and requires the vendor to 'demonstrate scalability beyond point solution.' On the 26 Feb 2026 call the near-term scope was confirmed as iSupplier + iRisk for incident management, with the full Source-to-Pay suite (direct and indirect spend) evaluated separately in a later phase. No line-item scoring rubric or weighting has been shared with the relevant stakeholder move is to ask the relevant stakeholder actual scoring criteria directly from Josh or Leslie before the S2P RFP lands.

items.name: Close date pushed ~6 weeks
items.summary: Close date moved from 2026-07-14 to 2026-08-27, a meaningful slip that signals delay on the buyer side or a timeline reset; no accompanying note explains the cause.

day_summary.overall: The Checkers - Rally's close date got pushed roughly six weeks, from late July to end of August. That's the only recorded activity today — no note explaining the reason, but a slip of this size usually means either a stalled internal process on their side or a re-scoping of the deal that isn't reflected yet. We're not shown as waiting on a specific deliverable, so the ball is effectively in our court to find out why and re-engage.

cro_panel.intro: This is an early, single-threaded deal marking time while it waits on a buyer-side RFP that has already slipped three times.; One real buyer call has occurred in five months, no economic buyer or budget conversation exists, and the deal's own history shows Zycus already lost a related evaluation once on fit grounds

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Buyer has been silent for 129 days since 26 Feb 2026 demo; no reply to repeated rep follow-ups; RFP overdue by a week with no update — deal may have moved to…
No EB identified, no DM identified, no CPO/CFO/CIO engagement — deal is single-threaded to two mid-level contacts who have gone dark

blocks.read: Going quiet — engagement has dropped off.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No named competitor exists for the still-unreleased S2P RFP.

bullets.text: S2P RFP has slipped three times (end-Apr → May → early-Jul) and is now a week overdue with no buyer update; close date 27 Aug is unattainable from a cold Qualified deal
No budget discussion, no EB engagement, no commercials — the $125k investment is unvalidated

items: Call or email Leslie McElhaney and Josh Ullrich to confirm S2P RFP release status, now overdue. (by 16 Jun)
Ask for a direct introduction to a senior sponsor (Gina Rendar, or Chris Ward/Michael Blair/Minh Le) to begin mapping an economic buyer. (by 23 Jun)
Log Leslie McElhaney, Josh Ullrich, and Gina Rendar as Opportunity Contact Roles in Salesforce. (by 23 Jun)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.detail: No economic buyer or decision-maker identified — MEDDPICC flags EB as a hard gap, and no exec has ever engaged Zycus.
Single-threaded relationship (Leslie + RFP-coordination contacts) with no backup path if she disengages.
S2P RFP has slipped three times and is now overdue by a week with no buyer update — the 8/27/26 close date is unattainable from a cold Qualified stage.
Zycus already lost the adjacent SIM RFP on 3/12/26 over 'fitment gaps too high,' raising real risk of a repeat outcome on the S2P evaluation.
Forecast category 'Best Case' is unsupported by evidence — no budget, no EB, no paper process — pointing to forecast inflation risk on a $125k deal.
Mitigant: buyer explicitly confirmed on 6/4/26 that Zycus remains included in the eventual RFP, rather than being dropped outright.

win_position.text: No economic buyer identified (MEDDPICC EB = gap); deal is single-threaded to Leslie McElhaney and RFP-coordination contacts with zero direct or indirect exec engagement. ► Get a warm intro to the CPO/CFO sponsor before the RFP re-releases.

No confirmed champion — Leslie has performed scheduling/timeline tasks only, with no advocacy evidence since the single 2/26/26 demo. ► Turn Leslie into a champion with a targeted ROI/business-case conversation.

S2P RFP has slipped three times and is now over a week overdue with no update, making the 8/27/26 close date unrealistic from a Qualified, pre-RFP stage. ► Confirm a firm release date directly with a buyer exec.

Zycus withdrew from the related SIM RFP on 3/12/26 citing 'fitment gaps too high' — a real signal of fit risk carrying into the broader S2P scope. ► Get ahead of scalability/fit objections before S2P submission.

No budget or commercial conversation on record; the $125k value and the 'Best Case' forecast category are unvalidated at Qualified stage. ► Validate budget and paper process before this forecast category is trusted.
Buyer proactively reaffirmed continued inclusion on 6/4/26 rather than dropping Zycus, keeping the door open despite the delays.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer meeting in 133 days since the single 2/26/26 demo — zero meetings logged in the 30/60/90-day windows. ► Book a live session tied to RFP release, not another email check-in.

Close date already pushed 44 days (7/14→8/27) and the S2P RFP itself has slipped three times (end-Apr→May→early-Jul), now overdue by a week with no update. ► Don't re-baseline close date until the RFP actually lands.
No future meeting or task is on the calendar — forward pipeline of activity is empty. ► Get a dated follow-up locked for RFP-release week.
Rep has driven every touch on this next-step trail (2/17 through 6/22); the only buyer-side response on record is Leslie's 6/4/26 note. ► Push for a two-way commitment, not a status update.
Buyer did confirm continued inclusion as recently as 6/4/26 ("S2P release pushed to Q3, we will still be included") — deal is thin, not dead.
The promised 'first week of July' RFP release date has now passed with no logged confirmation. ► Chase this specifically before it goes fully cold.

fit.ai_fit_signal: AI excitement in this deal has been Zycus-pitched rather than customer-pulled; the buyer's active RFP is scoped to supplier incident management, not an AI mandate.
fit.expectations_fit: Fit is being tested against evaluation pressure points Zycus itself has already logged: cost/timeline discipline, demonstrated scalability beyond a point solution, and implementation rigor.
fit.positioning_strength: Zycus demonstrated deep AI capability (Merlin agentic AI, Smart Onboarding OCR, QuestGenie scorecard builder) live on the 2026-02-26 call, and the buyer acknowledged Zycus's Gartner standing, but did not raise AI as a selection criterion th

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c=4.0, AIS_Status__c='Moderate'. The demo covered AI-driven supplier onboarding and KPI/scorecard automation, but the buyer's RFP scope and stated needs center on procurement-process transformation (structured incident ticketing), not an AI mandate. AI appetite is neither over- nor under-rated by the calls; it tracks the field.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (out-of-spec deliveries, fragmented incident feedback, manual Excel scorecarding) but no quantified cost, time, or ROI figures discussed (Avoma call 26 Feb 2026; SF Customer_Business_Problem__c).

Rep follow-up awaiting RFP release, expected first week of July; unanswered since

deal_movement.summary: Stage moved to Qualified the day the opp was created (11 Feb 2026) and has not advanced since (146 days). Amount and forecast category were set early (18 Feb 2026) and have not changed. Close date has slipped twice, entirely driven by the buyer's repeatedly delayed S2P RFP release; the last confirmed two-way buyer touch was 4 Jun 2026, with a rep follow-up on 22 Jun 2026 still unanswered as of today.

critical_signals.text: No named rival has surfaced, but Zycus itself opted out of the first-phase incident-management RFP in March over fit gaps, a self-inflicted risk that could resurface.
Leslie confirmed on 4 June that the bigger RFP was pushed to the start of Q3 but Zycus stays in the running, yet a follow-up two weeks ago asking for a release date has gone unanswered.
No new stakeholder has engaged since the February demo; three C-level Checkers contacts on file (supply chain, finance, IT) remain completely untouched.
Budget ownership and approval authority have never been discussed, so the $125k figure remains unvalidated against any real buying process.

confidence_signals.summary: One buyer touchpoint on record with rich pain/context evidence, but no confirmed two-way contact beyond a single 4 Jun 2026 status update in over four months; engagement is cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: 146 days at Qualified since creation (11 Feb 2026), no stage advance. Close date has slipped twice: 25 May 2026 -> 14 Jul 2026 (+50 days, moved 11 May 2026) -> 27 Aug 2026 (+44 days, moved 15 Jun 2026), a combined 94-day slip from the earliest recorded target. Last confirmed two-way buyer exchange was 4 Jun 2026 (33 days ago); the most recent rep follow-up (22 Jun 2026, 15 days ago) awaiting the 'first week of July' RFP release has gone unanswered. 51 days remain to the current 27 Aug close date, and the RFP that would trigger real evaluation has still not been confirmed released.
evidence: Next_Step_History__c 2026-06-04: 'Leslie response - S2P release pushed to beginning of Q3, we will still be included'
Next_Step_History__c 2026-06-22: 'awaiting RFP release first week of July' (no further update logged as of 7 Jul 2026)
OpportunityFieldHistory: StageName Initial Interest -> Qualified same day as creation (11 Feb 2026), no subsequent stage movement
north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is single-threaded to two operational Checkers contacts awaiting a Source-to-Pay RFP that has slipped three times and is now overdue past its last-stated window, with no economic buyer or champion in place.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (RFP not yet confirmed released, 0 contact roles, no EB or champion identified; Best Case is not supportable at Qualified stage 51 days out)

flags: Re-engage the buyer directly by phone (not email) — the deal has been silent for 129 days and the RFP is a week overdue; if no reply in 5 business days, disqualify or move to nurture.

Quantify the business case — the buyer described operational pain (lost incident emails, manual Excel tracking) but never stated the cost, FTE burden, or savings; build the ROI to justify the $125k investment.

Zycus self-withdrew from the SIM RFP on 12 Mar 2026 citing fitment gaps; pre-brief Josh or Leslie on how the full-suite scope resolves those gaps so Zycus isn't pre-judged as a fit risk again in the S2P round.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal has real, well-documented pain and an active RFP process, but is thin on access and unresolved from a prior self-elimination; three moves would materially raise win probability.

customer_preference.evidence: Gina expressed prior positive impression of Zycus and interest in revisiting full suite; prospect acknowledged Zycus as Gartner Magic Quadrant leader and customer choice 2025 (Avoma call 26 Feb 2026).

competitive_position.summary: No named vendor competitor has surfaced in any source (Competitors__c is blank, no Avoma call or Next_Step mentions a rival by name). The only competitive signal is Checkers' own RFP-process field noting a 'competitive vendor landscape with specialized point solutions' evaluated under strict cost/timeline criteria with third-party advisor Maine Pointe overseeing the process (SF Why_not_Zycus__c). Zycus itself self-eliminated from the first-phase Supplier Incident Management RFP on 12 Mar 2026, citing 'fitment gaps too high' (Next_Step__c), while remaining engaged for the larger Source-to-Pay suite RFP expected in Q3 2026. The strongest current threat is Zycus's own unresolved fit gap from the SIM round, plus the buyer's status-quo manual/Excel process, which could persist as a 'do nothing' outcome if the S2P RFP keeps slipping.

competitors.name: Status quo (manual Excel/email process)
competitors.quote: Incident and supplier performance tracked manually in Excel scorecards until system implementation
competitors.how_we_win: Tie the S2P RFP response directly to replacing the fragmented Excel/email process with structured ticketing and automated routing, using the buyer's own stated pain as the compelling event.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This is an early, single-threaded deal marking time while it waits on a buyer-side RFP that has already slipped three times. One real buyer call has occurred in five months, no economic buyer or budget conversation exists, and the deal's own history shows Zycus already lost a related evaluation once on fit grounds.

deal_risk.text: Zero contact roles are logged against this opportunity despite three named call attendees, leaving the record structurally single-threaded even where real contacts exist.
A $125,000 Best Case forecast rests on zero commercial discussion, zero budget confirmation, and zero economic-buyer contact - the forecast category is not defensible against the evidence.

win_position.text: No economic buyer is engaged and none has even been named among the senior Checkers contacts on file (CSCO Chris Ward, CFO Michael Blair, CIO Minh Le) - access to power is unmapped.

The deal is single-threaded to a supply chain manager (Josh Ullrich) and an unconfirmed RFP coordinator (Leslie McElhaney); the one more senior attendee, Gina Rendar, disengaged mid-call on 2026-02-26 and has not reappeared.
Zycus already self-withdrew from a related Supplier Incident Management RFP on 2026-03-12 over 'fitment gaps too high,' an unresolved credibility risk for the larger S2P evaluation still to come.

deal_momentum.text: Last verified two-way buyer engagement was the 2026-02-26 demo, 132-133 days ago; every entry since is a rep-logged follow-up (most recently 2026-06-22) awaiting a reply, not a confirmed buyer touch.

The full S2P RFP - the one live-wire event this deal is waiting on - has slipped from 'May' to 'beginning of Q3' (per a 2026-06-04 rep note) to 'first week of July,' and that most recent target has now passed by roughly a week with no confirmed release.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer did commit to sending an internal review and scheduling a live full-suite session for Gina Rendar after the 2026-02-26 demo, but neither has been confirmed as completed in the five months since.

items.quote: Requested overview of Zycus full spend management suite beyond incident management
items.requirement: Send pre-recorded demo video of full end-to-end S2P suite to Leslie McElhaney and Gina Rendar

items.deliverable: Release the full Source-to-Pay RFP to Zycus
items.grounding_quote: S2P release pushed to beginning of Q3, we will still be included

items.commitment: Confirm outcome/status of the Supplier Incident Management RFP award
items.deliverable: Confirm outcome/status of the Supplier Incident Management RFP award
items.grounding_quote: follow up with all 3 contacts at C&R, status of SIM award and S2P RFP release

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI capabilities (Merlin assistant, Smart Onboarding OCR, QuestGenie scorecards) were demonstrated on 26 Feb 2026, and the buyer acknowledged Zycus's Gartner leadership, but the buyer did not treat AI as a driving purchase criterion; it was Zycus-pitched, not customer-pulled.

items.quote: Requirement to demonstrate scalability beyond point solution
items.criterion: Demonstrate scalability beyond a point solution into full spend management
Strong implementation discipline and structured rollout capability

items.quote: Strict evaluation against cost and timeline criteria
items.criterion: Strict cost and timeline evaluation criteria

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Checkers' RFP-evaluation criteria (read from SF fields, no MEDDPICC 2.0 record exists) show partial alignment: Zycus's roadmap fits the scalability requirement but pricing/timeline rigor and implementation-discipline proof points remain untested.

hard.ais_why:
• Demo covered Merlin AI assistant, AI-driven supplier onboarding via Smart Onboarding OCR, and QuestGenie scorecard builder - Checkers acknowledged Gartner leadership but did not push AI as a buying criterion.
• The active RFP is scoped to Supplier Incident Management (iSupplier + iRisk), with the full S2P RFP deferred to May; current state is manual Excel scorecarding, not an AI mandate.
• One recorded demo, RFP imminent - solid procurement transformation deal, but AI excitement is Zycus-pitched rather than customer-pulled.
